coredata.dc:title: The lithium battery SOC estimation on square root unscented Kalman filter
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2022 The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:publisher: The Author(s). Published by Elsevier Ltd.
coredata.prism:issueName: The 2022 International Symposium on New Energy Technology Innovation and Low Carbon Development
coredata.dc:creator: LIU, Qinghe
YU, Quanqing
coredata.dc:description: Accurate state of charge (SOC) estimation is still one of the most important academic and application problems in the field of new energy so far. In order to improve SOC accuracy of lithium cobalt oxide battery, the authors obtained the SOC data with battery open circuit voltage (OCV) by charge/discharge experiments. Besides, the parameters of second-order RC equivalent circuit, including polarization capacitors and resistances, were identified through the rebound voltage test under different SOC condition. The model simulation data was carried out with MATLAB/Simulink by applying user-defined current. The simulation results whose error value was less than 25 mV fit the experimental data quite well and it well verified the correctness of the battery model and the accuracy of the parameters identifications. In this paper, a square root UKF algorithm was constructed Systematically on the lithium cobalt oxide battery model to estimate the SOC under complex conditions and environment. By comparing the SOC estimation results between traditional Kalman filter with the SR-UKF, the data indicated that SR-UKF algorithm our proposed can increase the robust of the filter and the estimation error can be reduced to less than 1.5%.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Lithium cobalt oxide battery
SOC estimation
Battery model
The square root unscented Kalman filter(SR-UKF)

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: The second-order Thevenin equivalent circuit model.
Comparison between simulation result and test result.
Comparison of two SOC estimation methods.
The error comparison under different SOC estimation methods.

ce:dochead.ce:textfn: 2022 International Symposium on New Energy Technology Innovation and Low Carbon Development (NET-LC 2022), January 21 to 23, 2022, Kunming, China

head.ce:title: The lithium battery SOC estimation on square root unscented Kalman filter

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Automotive Engineering, Harbin Institute of Technology at Weihai, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: School of Automotive Engineering, Harbin Institute of Technology at Weihai

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: School of Automotive Engineering, Harbin Institute of Technology at Weihai

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Accurate state of charge (SOC) estimation is still one of the most important academic and application problems in the field of new energy so far. In order to improve SOC accuracy of lithium cobalt oxide battery, the authors obtained the SOC data with battery open circuit voltage (OCV) by charge/discharge experiments. Besides, the parameters of second-order RC equivalent circuit, including polarization capacitors and resistances, were identified through the rebound voltage test under different SOC condition. The model simulation data was carried out with MATLAB/Simulink by applying user-defined current. The simulation results whose error value was less than 25 mV fit the experimental data quite well and it well verified the correctness of the battery model and the accuracy of the parameters identifications. In this paper, a square root UKF algorithm was constructed Systematically on the lithium cobalt oxide battery model to estimate the SOC under complex conditions and environment. By comparing the SOC estimation results between traditional Kalman filter with the SR-UKF, the data indicated that SR-UKF algorithm our proposed can increase the robust of the filter and the estimation error can be reduced to less than 1.5%.

ce:keyword.ce:text: The square root unscented Kalman filter(SR-UKF)

ce:def-description.ce:para: The OCV of the battery with SOC
The voltage of C1, C2 and R Ω

ce:section.ce:para: As the global environmental problems caused by atmospheric warming become more and more serious, countries all over the world take the new energy and low-carbon development as an important strategic task. Electric vehicle is considered to be an important solution to realize low-carbon transportation because it uses battery to replace internal combustion engine and achieves zero carbon emissions. However, battery life and reliability have always been important technical obstacles restricting the rapid popularization of electric vehicles, especially the accurate SOC estimation of battery is the key technical problem that has the greatest impact on them.

During the use of lithium-ion batteries in vehicle power system, thousands of battery cells are often assembled by parallel or series together. The problem of over-charge or over-discharge caused by the inconsistency of the single cells can cause the failure of the battery and even the safety accidents. Therefore, how to gain the SOC of each single cell in real time to avoid overcharge and over-discharge in the use process has become one of the most critical problems in the current electric vehicle power battery field.

The methods for SOC estimating are mainly divided into three types (1) the method based on Coulomb counting, (2) the method based on characterization parameters and (3) the method based on battery model and related observer [1].

The Coulomb counting SOC estimation is very accurate and there is no obvious side-effects during operation. However, this method must have the actual value of initial SOC, and after long period cycles, the measurement error will accumulate continuously, and additional error correction is needed.

The method based on battery characterization parameters is the most direct and effective method for SOC estimation [2,3]. The method mainly includes impedance spectroscopy, OCV method and so on. The merits of this methods are that the battery SOC can be obtained directly by mapping the SOC data along with battery characterization parameters. Besides, the shortcomings are that its application condition is limited, and it relies too much on the accuracy of the off-line relationship map between SOC and characterization parameters. For example, the OCV method needs a long time of static to obtain the OCV of battery, which is not suitable for SOC estimation in driving state.

The method based on battery model and related observers is the most popular SOC estimation method, including neural network, Kalman filter, and so on. He Jun [4] applied neural network algorithm whose input variables are the voltage difference and the current difference of the single cell, and the battery SOC is the output of the neural network. The weights of neural network are trained by genetic algorithm. This method needs large amount of data to simulate and improve the battery model.

There is no doubt that the most widely used SOC estimating method is Kalman filter till now. Many researchers try to study various methods to improve the accuracy of SOC estimation [5–7]. With the development of novel Kalman filter, the estimating accuracy of SOC has also been significantly improved. KC Lim et al. [8] proposed the annealed KF to estimate the battery SOC. H He et al. [9] used UKF to estimate the battery SOC in real time. J Meng [10] used adaptive UKF to estimate SOC.
In order to solve the non-positive covariance problem in the running process of the unscented Kalman filter, this paper introduces the SR-UKF to enhance the robustness.

High accuracy of battery model is the basis of SOC estimating. After years of development, lots of models have been established which can be divided into three kinds according to the different emphasis: (1) the theory model focus on electrochemical characteristics, (2) the neural network model focus on input/output characteristics and (3) equivalent circuit model focus on battery external performance.

In this paper, a second-order RC equivalent circuit model is established, which is shown in Fig. 1. UOC(SOC) represents the OCV of the battery which has a specific function relation with the battery SOC under certain temperature conditions.
R
Ω
represents ohmic internal resistance. C1 and C2 are polarization capacitors. R1 and R2 are polarized internal resistances.

The model fully considers the relation among OCV, the SOC and resilience voltage characteristics of the battery. The mathematical relation expression of the model can be derived from the battery model as following.

The charge and discharge test of lithium cobalt battery under different degrees of SOC was carried out according to literature [11], and the parameters of the model were obtained by the least square method to identify the parameters, such as Tables 1 and 2.

In order to study the model accuracy, it is tested by multiple battery charging/discharge process which is shown in Fig. 2-a. It is can be seen that the current direction and value is changed frequently. The load can simulate the real running state of vehicle very well. The comparison between testing and simulating voltage is shown in Fig. 2-b and the fitting error is shown in Fig. 2-c.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The square root unscented Kalman filter
ce:section.ce:para: Kalman filter has obvious advantages compared with other SOC estimation methods. Firstly, Kalman filter can eliminate the initial SOC error of the battery after a short time correction. Secondly, it can eliminate the error of sensor measurement and the Gauss white noise.

Extended Kalman filter (EKF) can linearize and filter the nonlinear system, which solved the disadvantage that Kalman filter could not filter the nonlinear system. But there were still some problems in EKF [12]. EKF estimates the state distribution by Gauss random variables, and then updates by linearizing the nonlinear system, which may lead to a larger deviation of the posterior estimation, covariance and Gauss random variables, resulting in the failure to obtain the optimal solution and even make the filter discrete. The UKF is proposed to solve this technical barrier [13]. UKF can be regarded as a Kalman transform based on UT transform. The nonlinear transformation of mean and covariance in time update is handled by UT transform.

Untraced Kalman filter can estimate the battery SOC well, but the error covariance negative definite problem is sometimes encountered in the early operation, which eventually leads to the failure of the filter. The negative covariance is usually caused by iterative calculation and the noise signal [14]. Therefore, we use SR-UKF to estimate the SOC of battery, in which square root of covariance was used to substitute for the covariance to serve the divergence problem in covariance calculation.

According to Eqs. (1), the current is the input of UKF filter, the terminal voltage is taken as the observation variable which can be measured directly by senors. The SOC and the voltages
V
1
,
V
2
will change with the current during charge and discharge process. So, We set

, U , − 1 )
The two SOC estimation methods introduced in this paper are verified by test and simulation. The experimental object is 8.3Ah cobalt lithium battery.

The true SOC value was measured by the following methods. Firstly, the battery was fully stationary for 30 min, then the OCV of the battery was measured, and the accurate initial SOC value was obtained by looking up the SOC-OCV table. A customized working condition was applied to the battery by a special instrument, and the SOC curve with time was obtained by using a high precision current sensor and an ampere-hour integral method. This curve can serve as a benchmark for other algorithms. The Simulink model of the ampere integral method is shown in Fig. 3.

The data of current and voltage obtained by experiments are imported into SOC estimation program of EKF and SR-UKF respectively. The estimated values are compared with the SOC obtained by ampere-hour integration method which is shown in Fig. 4.

From Fig. 4, it is shown that SR-UKF SOC estimation methods can achieve higher accuracy than EKF methods compared with SOC exact value. The error of EKF SOC methods increases with the discharge time, mainly because EKF requires higher accuracy of battery model parameters relative to SR-UKF. Comparatively, the accuracy of SU-UKF SOC estimation method keeps high level, and the precision is not degraded with the increase of charging and discharging time.

The SOC estimation error of EKF and SR-UKF methods is shown in Fig. 5. Compared with EKF, SR-UKF has higher precision and smaller error fluctuation. The maximum error of SR-UKF is less than 1.5% while the error of EKF is less than 2%.

In this paper, the equivalent circuit model of lithium cobalt oxide battery is modeled, and the parameters of the model are identified by experiment data. Based on the analysis of the advantages and disadvantages of two KF methods of EKF and SR-UKF, SOC estimation of lithium cobalt oxide battery is carried out. Because EKF is based on the linearization of nonlinear systems, ignoring higher order terms in Taylor expansion inevitably leads to errors, while UKF does not need to linearize nonlinear systems. To solve the problem of covariance non-positive definiteness in UKF operation, SR-UKF is introduced to enhance the robustness of the filter in this paper. The results show that the methods in this paper achieve relatively higher SOC estimation accuracy compared with the existing literature. The SOC estimation error of SR-UKF is less than 1.5% and that of EKF is less than 2% on high model accuracy.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Towards a smarter battery management system for electric vehicle applications: A critical review of lithium-ion battery state of charge estimation
Energies

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Muhammad Umair Ali, Amad Zafar, Sarvar Hussain Nengroo, others, 2019. Towards a Smarter Battery Management System for Electric Vehicle Applications: A Critical Review of Lithium-Ion Battery State of Charge Estimation, energies 12 (3) 445-478.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: State of charge dependency of graphitized-carbon-based reactions in a lithium-ion secondary cell studied by electrochemical impedance spectroscopy
Electrochim Acta

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Mendoza-Hernandez O S, Ishikawa H, Nishikawa Y, others, 2014. State of Charge Dependency of Graphitized-Carbon-Based Reactions in a Lithium-ion Secondary Cell Studied by Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy. Electrochimica Acta 113 (2) 168-173.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Harmonic impedance measurement based on-line estimation for SOC of storage battery
Electron Meas Technol

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Hu Yimin, Cao Yajie, Jing Gang, Liu Yan. 2015. Harmonic impedance measurement based on-line estimation for SOC of storage battery. Electronic measurement technology 38 (7) 94-96

sb:title.sb:maintitle: State of charge estimation of lithium-ion batteries using the open-circuit voltage at various ambient temperatures
Appl Energy

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Xing, others, 2014. State of Charge Estimation of Lithium-Ion Batteries Using the Open-Circuit Voltage at Various Ambient Temperatures. Applied Energy 113 (5) 106-115

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Extended Kalman filter based battery state of charge (SOC) estimation for electric vehicles
Transp Electrif Conf Expo

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Jiang Chenguan, Taylor A, Duan Chen, others, 2013. Extended Kalman Filter Based Battery State of Charge (SOC) Estimation for Electric Vehicles, Transportation Electrification Conference and Expo, 1-5.

sb:title.sb:maintitle: On-line battery identification for electric driving range prediction
Vehicle Power and Propulsion Conference (VPPC)

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Kessels J, Rosca B, Bergveld H J, others, 2011. On-line Battery Identification for Electric Driving Range Prediction, Vehicle Power and Propulsion Conference (VPPC), 1-6

sb:title.sb:maintitle: A comparative study of state of charge estimation algorithms for LiFePO4 batteries used in electric vehicles
J Power Sources

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Li Jiahao, Barillas J K, Guenther C, others, 2013. A Comparative Study of State of Charge Estimation Algorithms for LiFePO4 Batteries Used in Electric Vehicles, Journal of power sources 230 (2) 244-250

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Fading Kalman filter-based real-time state of charge estimation in LiFePO4 battery-powered electric vehicles
Appl Energy

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Lim K C, Bastawrous H A, Duong V H, others, 2016. Fading Kalman Filter-based Real-time State of Charge Estimation in LiFePO4 Battery-powered Electric Vehicles. Applied Energy, 169 (1) 40-48

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Real-time estimation of battery state-of-charge with unscented Kalman filter and RTOS μ cos-II platform
Appl Energy

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: He Hongwen, Xiong Rui, Peng Jiankun. 2016. Real-time Estimation of Battery State-of-charge with Unscented Kalman Filter and RTOS μCOS-II Platform, Applied Energy, 162 (2) 1410-1418

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Lithium polymer battery state-of-charge estimation based on adaptive unscented Kalman filter and support vector machine
IEEE Trans Power Electron

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Meng Jinhao, Luo Guangzhao, Gao Fei. 2016. Lithium Polymer Battery State-of-Charge Estimation Based on Adaptive Unscented Kalman Filter and Support Vector Machine. IEEE Transactions on Power Electronics, 31 (3) 2226-2238

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Modeling of high power automotive batteries by the use of an automated test system
IEEE Trans Instrum Meas

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Schweighofer B, Raab K M, Brasseur G. 2003. Modeling of High Power Automotive Batteries by the Use of an Automated Test System, IEEE Transactions on Instrumentation and Measurement, 52 (4) 1087-1091

sb:title.sb:maintitle: New extension of the Kalman filter to nonlinear systems
Signal Process Sensor Fusion Target Recognit VI

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Julier S J, Uhlmann J K. 1999. New Extension of the Kalman Filter to Nonlinear Systems, Signal Processing Sensor Fusion & Target Recognition VI, 306 (8) 182-193

sb:title.sb:maintitle: Application of UKf and SRUKF to power system dynamic state estimation
Proc Chin Soc Electr Eng

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: Wei Zhinong, Sun Guoqiang, Pang Bo. 2011. Application of UKF and SRUKF to Power System Dynamic State Estimation, Proceedings of the Chinese Society of Electrical Engineering, 31 (16) 74-80

sb:title.sb:maintitle: An algorithm for measurement matrix based on QR decomposition and eigen-value optimization
CAAI Trans Intell Syst

ce:bib-reference.ce:source-text: ZHENG Xiao, BO Hua, SUN Qiang. 2015. An algorithm for measurement matrix based on QR decomposition and eigen-value optimization, CAAI Transactions on Intelligent Systems, 10 (1) 149-154
